title: VBRC Base By Base: Import genome sequences from Virology.ca viral databases
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"887k6","text":"Viral Bioinformatic Resource Centre","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"caqli","text":"Provide databases of viral genomic information.  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":47}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"djani","text":"Please check the Organisms menu to see which viruses we support: we’re now focusing on large DNA viruses","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":17,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":17,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"96cfl","text":"
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"24fg9","text":"The VOCs (Virus Orthologous Clusters) database is at the heart of our system.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"de0u9","text":"
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e878l","text":"The database links directly to integrated tools for comparative analyses.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aat96","text":"
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7np6","text":"VOCs sorts genes into ortholog clusters (e.g. RNA polymerase) to simplify data retrieval.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6oj1u","text":"
  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82l52","text":"Provide easy access to the genes, gene families, and genomes of the different virus families.  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":93}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e9e2q","text":"via a unique series of powerful Java tools that support multiple computer platforms (see VBRC Tools menu).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":89,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":89,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5c06o","text":"

  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3v52","text":"Rally the research community to provide expert curation of these viral genomes by:  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":82}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6jq7a","text":"Adding value to GenBank sequences through enhancing and updating genome annotations","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4qhre","text":"

steps: From the “File” menu, choose “Add Sequences to Alignment” .
Click OK to upload the sequences to your alignment. (Note that these sequences will be unaligned).
“From VOCs Database” . Select the database and, from the list that appears, your genomes of interest (hold down the Apple key ( Mac ) or CTRL ( PC ) to select multiple genomes).
To view the file you just created, close the current alignment and open your new file.
creator: Nick Tang
